title: Creating Nodes
description: Creating nodes with labels and properties in Neo4j
code:
// Create a single node
CREATE (p:Person {name: 'Alice', age: 25, city: 'New York'})
RETURN p

// Create multiple nodes
CREATE (p1:Person {name: 'Bob'}),
 (p2:Person {name: 'Charlie'}),
 (c:Company {name: 'TechCorp'})
RETURN p1, p2, c
explanation: CREATE clause instantiates new nodes. Labels follow colon (:), properties in curly braces {}. RETURN displays results.

title: Creating Relationships
code:
// Match existing nodes and create relationship
MATCH (a:Person {name: 'Alice'}),
 (b:Person {name: 'Bob'})
CREATE (a)-[r:FRIEND {since: 2020, type: 'close'}]->(b)
RETURN a, r, b

// Create bidirectional relationship
MATCH (a:Person), (b:Person)
WHERE a.name = 'Alice' AND b.name = 'Charlie'
CREATE (a)-[:KNOWS]->(b), (b)-[:KNOWS]->(a)
explanation: MATCH finds existing nodes, CREATE adds relationships. Relationships can have properties. Arrow direction matters.

title: Reading and Filtering Data
description: Querying nodes and relationships with filters
code:
// Find all Person nodes
MATCH (p:Person)
RETURN p

// Find with property filter
MATCH (p:Person {name: 'Alice'})
RETURN p

// Find using WHERE clause
MATCH (p:Person)
WHERE p.age > 25 AND p.city = 'New York'
RETURN p.name, p.age

// Find with relationship
MATCH (a:Person)-[:FRIEND]->(b:Person)
WHERE a.name = 'Alice'
RETURN b.name
explanation: MATCH finds patterns, WHERE adds conditions. Can filter on node properties, labels, or relationship properties.

title: Updating Nodes and Relationships
code:
// Update node property
MATCH (p:Person {name: 'Alice'})
SET p.age = 26
RETURN p

// Add new property
MATCH (p:Person {name: 'Alice'})
SET p.email = 'alice@email.com'
RETURN p

// Remove property
MATCH (p:Person {name: 'Alice'})
REMOVE p.city
RETURN p

// Update relationship property
MATCH (a:Person)-[r:FRIEND]->(b:Person)
WHERE a.name = 'Alice'
SET r.since = 2019
RETURN r
explanation: SET modifies or adds properties, REMOVE deletes properties. Can update both nodes and relationship properties.

title: Deleting Nodes and Relationships
explanation: DELETE removes relationships, DETACH DELETE removes node and all its relationships. Always delete relationships first or use DETACH.

title: Complex Graph Traversal
description: Advanced queries traversing multiple hops in graph
code:
// Find friends of friends (2 hops)
MATCH (a:Person {name: 'Alice'})-[:FRIEND]->(b:Person)-[:FRIEND]->(c:Person)
WHERE NOT c.name = 'Alice'
RETURN DISTINCT c.name

// Find shortest path
MATCH (a:Person {name: 'Alice'}),
 (b:Person {name: 'Eve'}),
 path = shortestPath((a)-[*]-(b))
RETURN path

// Count relationships
MATCH (p:Person {name: 'Alice'})-[r:FRIEND]->()
RETURN count(r) AS friendCount
explanation: Use [*] for variable length paths, shortestPath() finds minimum hops, aggregate functions like count() work with relationships.

title: Social Network Query Examples
description: Practical queries for a social network application
code:
// Create sample social graph
CREATE (alice:Person {name: 'Alice'}),
 (bob:Person {name: 'Bob'}),
 (charlie:Person {name: 'Charlie'}),
 (david:Person {name: 'David'}),
 (alice)-[:FRIEND {since: 2018}]->(bob),
 (bob)-[:FRIEND {since: 2019}]->(charlie),
 (alice)-[:FOLLOWS]->(charlie),
 (david)-[:FRIEND {since: 2020}]->(bob)

// Who does Alice's friends know?
MATCH (alice:Person {name: 'Alice'})-[:FRIEND]->(friend)-[:FRIEND]->(foaf)
WHERE NOT foaf.name = 'Alice'
RETURN foaf.name AS suggestedFriend, count(*) AS times
ORDER BY times DESC
LIMIT 5
explanation: Real-world social network queries showing friend recommendations based on mutual friends (FOAF - Friend of a Friend).
